name: Or HaGanuz (Hidden Light)
shortDescription: Or HaGanuz, meaning 'Hidden Light' in Hebrew, refers to a primordial, divine light concealed within the Godhead in Kabbalistic thought. It is the source of all creation and a state of pure potentiality.
description: Or HaGanuz is a central concept in Kabbalah, representing the infinite, unmanifest light of the Ein Sof (the Infinite). Before the creation of the universe, this light filled all of existence. In order to allow for creation and the emergence of finite beings, God contracted (Tzimtzum) this light, creating a void into which the universe could unfold. However, a residue of this original, hidden light remains within all things, providing them with their spiritual essence and connection to the divine source. This hidden light is not directly perceivable but can be accessed through spiritual practices and mystical insight. It represents the potential for redemption and the ultimate reunification of all creation with the Ein Sof. The concept is related to the Ner Tamid (eternal light) in synagogues, symbolizing the constant presence of this divine spark. It also echoes similar concepts of hidden or underlying realities in other spiritual traditions, such as the concept of Brahman in Hinduism or the Tao in Taoism, where a fundamental, unmanifest principle underlies all manifest phenomena. The Etz Chaim (Tree of Life) in Kabbalah further illustrates the emanation of this hidden light through the Sefirot, the divine attributes that shape the universe.

sources.title: The Wisdom of the Zohar: An Anthology
sources.author: Isaiah Tishby
